question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here are all the hook events a Juju charm can observe:

## Lifecycle Hooks
- **install** — Run once when the unit is first deployed
- **config-changed** — Run whenever configuration changes (and after install)
- **start** — Run after install and config-changed on initial deployment
- **stop** — Run when the unit is about to be destroyed
- **remove** — Run when the unit is being torn down
- **upgrade-charm** — Run when the charm code is upgraded

## Relation Hooks
For each relation `<name>` defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<name\>-relation-created** — A new relation has been created
- **\<name\>-relation-joined** — A new unit has joined the relation
- **\<name\>-relation-changed** — Relation data has changed
- **\<name\>-relation-departed** — A unit is leaving the relation
- **\<name\>-relation-broken** — The relation is being removed entirely

## Storage Hooks
For each storage `<name>` defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<name\>-storage-attached** — Storage has been attached to the unit
- **\<name\>-storage-detaching** — Storage is about to be detached

## Leader Hooks
- **leader-elected** — This unit has become the leader
- **leader-settings-changed** — Leader settings have changed (deprecated in favour of relation data)

## Action Hooks
For each action `<name>` defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<name\>-action** — The action has been invoked by a user

## Secret Hooks
- **secret-changed** — A secret the charm is tracking has a new revision
- **secret-expired** — A secret owned by the charm has expired
- **secret-rotate** — A secret owned by the charm needs rotation
- **secret-remove** — An old secret revision can be removed

## Pebble / Container Hooks (Kubernetes charms only)
For each container `<name>` defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<name\>-pebble-ready** — The Pebble agent in the container is ready
- **\<name\>-pebble-custom-notice** — A custom Pebble notice has been fired

## Other
- **collect-unit-status** — Called before Juju reports status; lets the charm set unit status
- **collect-app-status** — Called before Juju reports status; lets the charm set app status (leader only)
- **update-status** — Fired periodically (default every 5 minutes) for health checks
- **pre-series-upgrade** / **post-series-upgrade** — Run during OS series upgrades (machine charms, legacy)

In Ops, you observe these using `self.on.<event_name>` or via the framework's event names (e.g. `self.framework.observe(self.on.install, self._on_install)`). The `collect-status` events are typically handled via `self.unit.status` in the collect handler.
